input_dict.question: Between Jason Bateman and Jan Troell, which filmmaker has had a more diverse career?
input_dict.source_summary: Neither is clearly more diverse — both have comparably varied careers. Jan Gustaf Troell usually writes his own scripts, serves as his own director of photography, and directs films. Jason Kent Bateman has worked as an actor, director, and producer.
input_dict.target_summary:
Jan Gustaf Troell (born 23 July 1931) — Swedish film director. (Gold-supported: “Jan Gustaf Troell ... is a Swedish film director.”) Summary-level facts: Troell usually writes his own scripts and serves as his own director of photography. Films directed by Troell mentioned in the inputs include Hurricane (1979), Hamsun (1996), Everlasting Moments (2008), and Land of Dreams (Sagolandet) (filming from 1983–1986).

Jason Kent Bateman (born January 14, 1969) — American actor, director, and producer. Summary-level facts: Bateman began acting on television in the early 1980s (Little House on the Prairie, Silver Spoons, The Hogan Family), is known for the role Michael Bluth in Arrested Development (won a Golden Globe and a Satellite Award), has had starring film roles, and led the 2017 Netflix series Ozark. Additional film appearances in the inputs include Breaking the Rules (1992) and Disconnect (2012).

These integrated facts preserve the specific occupations and notable credits for each person (Troell: director, screenwriter, serves as his own director of photography; Bateman: actor, director, producer, TV and film performer) so a downstream final-answer agent can use them to assess career diversity without this summary asserting a comparative judgement.
